samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

The key `core.example` isn't set — the CLI reports:

```
luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

**How configuration works here (Lantern):**

Config is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two Tiers:

- **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone on the project.
- **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no `--level` writes to personal by default, so casual changes never land in shared state. You'd need `--level team` to explicitly write the shared file.

Separately, each config file can carry a free-form Markdown body (in `.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first. Context can also be scoped to a single skill via files under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/`. Editing these bodies is a manual, user-driven action rather than something done through `luminosity config`.

luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

**How configuration works here (Lantern):**

Config is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two Tiers:

- **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone on the project.
- **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no `--level` writes to personal by default, so casual changes never land in shared state. You'd need `--level team` to explicitly write the shared file.

Separately, each config file can carry a free-form Markdown body (in `.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first. samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

The key `core.example` isn't set:

```
luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

Regarding how configuration (Beacon) works in this project:

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs resolved across two levels — **team** (shared, committed file) and **personal** (local, git-ignored, overrides team). A plain `get`/`set` without `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team.
- Separate from key/value config, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — and skills can additionally have their own scoped context files under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/`.
- I only interact with the values via the `luminosity config` CLI — I don't read/parse the files directly, and body edits are left to you since those are user-owned.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
The key `core.example` isn't set — `luminosity config get core.example` exited non-zero with:

```
luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

**How configuration works in this project** (this project calls it **Lantern**):

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs resolved across two **Tiers**:
  - **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone
  - **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key
- A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no `--level` writes personal by default, so casual changes never land in shared state.
- Separate from these CLI-owned values, each level also has a free-form Markdown **body** (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — those bodies are edited by hand, not through the CLI.

Since `core.example` isn't set at either level, want me to set it (and if so, at which tier)?

The key `core.example` isn't set — `luminosity config get core.example` exited non-zero with:

```
luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

**How configuration works in this project** (this project calls it **Lantern**):

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs resolved across two **Tiers**:
  - **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone
  - **personal** — local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key
- A `get` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no `--level` writes personal by default, so casual changes never land in shared state.
- Separate from these CLI-owned values, each level also has a free-form Markdown **body** (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — those bodies are edited by hand, not through the CLI.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

The `core.example` key isn't set — the CLI exited non-zero with: `luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set`.

On how configuration (Beacon) works in this project:

- Config is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two levels: **team** (shared, committed) and **personal** (local, git-ignored, overrides team). A plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team.
- Separate from these CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first.
- Context can also be scoped to one skill via `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/context.md` (team) and `context.local.md` (personal), injected only into that skill's prompt.
- I only read/write the dotted values through the `luminosity config` CLI — I don't touch the config files directly, and editing the Markdown bodies is left to you.

On how configuration (Beacon) works in this project:

- Config is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two levels: **team** (shared, committed) and **personal** (local, git-ignored, overrides team). A plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves personal-over-team.
- Separate from these CLI-owned values, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` for team, `.luminosity/config.local.md` for personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — team and personal bodies combine, team-first.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
The key `core.example` is not set — the CLI exited non-zero with:

```
luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set
```

**How configuration works here (Lantern):**

Config is a tree of dotted `section.key` values resolved across two Tiers:

- **team** — the shared, committed file (`.luminosity/config.md` frontmatter), visible to everyone on the project.
- **personal** — the local, git-ignored file (`.luminosity/config.local.md` frontmatter), which overrides team for matching keys.

A plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team, so reads see the effective value and writes never accidentally land in shared state.

Separately, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body beneath its frontmatter. That body isn't a key/value — it's injected as project context into every skill's prompt (team and personal bodies combine, team-first), plus there's a further skill-specific scoping layer for context that should only apply to one particular skill.

messages.content:
The key `core.example` isn't set — the CLI exited non-zero with: `luminosity: config key 'core.example' is not set`.

On how configuration works here: this project uses **Beacon**, a two-level config system managed entirely through the `luminosity config` CLI (I don't read/write the files directly). Values are dotted `section.key` pairs resolved across:

- **team** — shared, committed file, visible to everyone
- **personal** — local, git-ignored file, overrides team for the same key

A plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team, so accidental changes never land in shared state. Separately, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body (`.luminosity/config.md` team / `.luminosity/config.local.md` personal) that gets injected as project context into every skill's prompt — and skills can additionally have their own scoped context files (e.g. `.luminosity/skills/configure/context.md`). Those bodies are user-edited directly, not something I write to.
